symbols: star above with angel — the Star of Bethlehem guided by divine providence
rocky cave setting — the humble grotto of the Nativity
kneeling elderly Magus — the wisdom of the world submitted to divine wisdom
camels and retinue — the long journey from the East, the nations coming to the light
gold vessels and gifts — gold, frankincense, and myrrh for king, God, and sacrifice
description: A large, elaborate composition set in a rocky mountainous landscape. At the right, the Virgin Mary sits holding the Christ Child in a cave-like grotto, with Joseph nearby and angels hovering above. An elderly Magus kneels before them, kissing or presenting a gift. Behind, a long procession of richly dressed figures, soldiers, camels, and horses winds up a rocky path from the left. At the upper right, angels and a bright star appear on the hillside. The scene is densely populated with figures in elaborate costumes and rendered with meticulous detail.
theological_description: Mantegna's Adoration of the Magi presents the Epiphany as a grand cosmic event. The Magi's procession winding through the rocky landscape represents the pilgrimage of the nations toward the light of Christ. Matthew records that they followed a star from the East to Bethlehem (MAT.2.1-2), fulfilling Balaam's oracle: 'A star shall come out of Jacob' (NUM.24.17). The cave setting follows the ancient tradition, attested in the Protoevangelium of James and by Justin Martyr, that Christ was born in a cave — connecting the Nativity to the burial cave and thus linking birth to death and resurrection. The three gifts are interpreted christologically: gold for his kingship, frankincense for his divinity, and myrrh for his burial (MAT.2.11). Isaiah's prophecy is fulfilled: 'Nations shall come to your light, and kings to the brightness of your rising. They shall bring gold and frankincense' (ISA.60.3-6). The elaborate retinue emphasizes that the humble birth in a cave is simultaneously the most significant event in cosmic history.

scripture_refs.reason: The complete narrative of the Magi following the star and presenting gifts
Nations coming to the light, bringing gold and frankincense
A star out of Jacob — Balaam's prophecy fulfilled in the Star of Bethlehem
Kings of Tarshish bringing gifts, all kings falling before him
Out of Bethlehem shall come a ruler — the destination of the Magi's journey
